title: The Two-Minute Money Minute
subtitle: A daily ritual for building a steadier relationship with money, one short practice at a time
excerpt: A two-minute daily ritual for anyone who wants to start money work but doesn't have time for a long practice. One breath, one statement, one intention. Works as a standalone starter or as a daily anchor alongside longer practices.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education as a beginner daily anchor for money work.

practice.whenToUse: Use daily, before checking your phone or your bank account. Takes two minutes. Works as a standalone or alongside a longer morning practice.

content.text: A daily two-minute ritual for meeting money from a steadier place before the day starts. Not a full ceremony. Not a deep practice. A short daily habit that, done consistently, shifts the baseline.
Sit upright. Take one slow breath in through your nose, hold for a count of three, and let it out through your mouth. Feel your feet on the floor.
Say one of the following aloud, hand on chest. Pick the one that fits today, or rotate through them across the week:
Money is not a threat. It is a resource I am learning to work with.
Today I meet money with steadiness rather than fear.
I am building a different relationship with money, one day at a time.
Name one money-adjacent thing you will do today from a place of steadiness rather than anxiety. It does not have to be large. Open the app. Transfer the ten pounds. Reply to the email.
Written for homemade.education as a daily beginner anchor for money work.
